Bennet understands the startup mentality, the entrepreneurship mindset, what makes these companies tick, and the risks that people are comfortable with. Within the Shell Ventures group, he always keeps this perspective, but on the other hand, having been consulting large companies for a long time, and having gone through Shell’s foundational and cultural on boarding and training, Bennet has become a bridge \- who can speak both languages. 

Bennett has identified the following elements of *voodoo* to be important in being a successful Corporate Venture Capitalist:

* Understanding the the start-up mentality as well as the corporate mentality  
* Having extensive domain expertise  
* Being open minded and curious


#### Perspective from Arrowhead Innovation Fund: Regional Economic Development in Partnership with a University {#perspective-from-arrowhead-innovation-fund:-regional-economic-development-in-partnership-with-a-university}

Estela Hernandez had a career path that has included many different things including a health educator, design thinking guru and venture investor. These unique experiences have shaped her perspective in the venture space and have given her a kind of patience that is sometimes rare in the space. According to her, “I’ve had to take a couple of steps back sometimes to take a couple of steps forward.”

Estela Hernandez, her mentor, and stakeholders at New Mexico State University, created an early-stage microfund to serve the NMSU community and to foster regional economic development. The fund seeks to invest in companies that are being spun out of New Mexico State University or affiliates of NMSU, which include alumni (or even spouse of an alumni), and participants in some of the incubator programs at NMSU.

Estela has learned to build on the region’s strengths rather than copying the Silicon Valley blueprint. The fund builds on the region's strengths and focuses on agricultural technology, aerospace design, water and energy technologies to name a few of the industries. The ecosystem is ripe to support this type of development given the economy’s needs. Then after a company raises their seed round, the Arrowhead Innovation Fund’s goal is help find the resources in the region and outside to help the company grow. Knowing that their fund can help provide the seed funding, Estela and her team also understand, that sometimes when the company grows, it might have to leave the region.

### Perspectives from Service Providers {#perspectives-from-service-providers}

#### Perspectives from Live Oak Bank on Adding Value for Entrepreneurs  {#perspectives-from-live-oak-bank-on-adding-value-for-entrepreneurs}

In an industry built heavily upon positive relationships, Live Oak Bank stresses the importance in understanding ways you can add value to entrepreneurs, even if you are passing on working with them. Erin Andrew provides explains that venture funding may not be the best first option for many startups. Being able to explain the multitude of options these companies have for raising funds or acquiring loans to continue with their idea will not only add value to entrepreneurs, but will create lasting connections that will benefit your firm in the future. 

Erin believes that companies that can leverage debt in a way that makes sense, may be in better shape to take on an equity investment in the future. Understanding the   
significant opportunity between the debt and equity space will have a lasting impact on individual companies and the industry as a whole.

Additionally, firms should have an understanding of the resources within the federal government for innovation. The Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) provides grants that can be the right option for individuals who are looking for funding and ways to get involved in solving challenges within the government. The Small Business Innovation Challenge (SBIC) also provides an opportunity to innovators to acquire financing for ideas that will impact individuals in a positive way. 

#### Perspectives from First Republic Bank on Supporting Emerging Managers and Community Development {#perspectives-from-first-republic-bank-on-supporting-emerging-managers-and-community-development}

First Republic Bank is trying to be so much more than a bank. They believe they are trying to create a platform to support Emerging Managers. A co-founder of FRB is female, Katherine August-Dewilt, so diversity of thought and perspectives has been a key cultural value of the organization. 

Typical banking services for venture capital firms include

* Capital Call Lines of Credit: a loan to the VC fund, borrowed against LP commitments and LP base (typically a ratio of uncalled capital). It bridges between making investments and allows efficiency around LP capital call management  
* Management Company Lines of Credit: a loan to the management company, borrowed against future management fees. It’s a revolving line of credit that helps with operational expenses, including salaries, rent, any other overhead costs   
* GP Contribution Lines of Credit: a term loan (interest only) that helps partners to make their GP commitment, borrowed against future management fees. It bridges the gap for GPs to fulfill their required commitment to the fund (GP commitment)  
* Fund Banking: NO banking fees for any of the firm accounts—100% of fees are waived; single point of contact throughout the life of the relationship and a dedicated team on the operations side  
* Personal Banking for GPs and Firm Employees:  
  * Mortgages  
  * Refinancing Student Loans  
  * Private Wealth Management  
* Employee Benefits  
  * Student Loan Repayment Programs

Key areas of the Emerging Manager support platform are fundraising, infrastructure building, data and insights, branding, and community. 

* **Fundraising:** provides help with deck reviews and fundraising strategies. Also, relationships built over the last 8 years with institutional LPs has helped the First Republic Emerging Manager team connect them to some of the best emerging managers, helping managers collectively raise $150 million. As some funds have moved to even more pre-seed stack/proof of concept stage, First Republic is building relationships with and providing targeted LP Workshops to Family Offices and high net worth individuals interested in venture investing.  
* **Infrastructure:** partnership with Strut Consulting to help managers with their firm’s operational infrastructure build out. First Republic has built a channel of infrastructure partners with preferred pricing for services such as design work, PR, Fund Admin, Tax/Audit, Insurance, etc.  
* **Data and insights:** access to industry data, including in the emerging managers for venture capital, regularly publishes data and insights on pevcbanker.com   
* **Branding:** with a dedicated site, innovation.firstrepublic.com, First Republic regularly publishes client spotlights (with their in house ghostwriters doing all the work after interviewing you\!) and invests additional dollars to push out the content in high traffic, relevant channels  
* **Community:** hosts intimate and curated GP and LP dinners and strategic events throughout the year, such as the RAISE GP/LP summit on emerging managers; also leading the charge with the women in venture (“WIV”) community building efforts.
